core_concept: Animal Life Cycles: Birth, Growth, Adult
facts: A kitten is born as a tiny, blind, and deaf baby that cannot walk, but it grows into an adult cat that can see, hear, and hunt because the kitten goes through a growth stage called development.
A chick hatches from an egg as a yellow, fluffy baby that looks very different from its parents, who have feathers, because the chick must grow and change before becoming an adult.
A puppy grows into a dog; adult dogs can have puppies of their own, which is part of the life cycle where young become adults and then have offspring.
misconceptions: A baby animal looks exactly like its parent, just smaller.
